pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh (line 16), export_result.sh (lines 5-6), and verifier.py logic. Consistent across all task scripts.
Verified in setup_task.sh line 197 where file is written. Path stored in scenario_data and written to INI file with proper structure.

Web search for Earth's mean radius. Standard value is 6371 km / 1.852 = 3440.065 nautical miles. Task value of 3440.06 is a correct rounding. Verified by testing Haversine formula against known great circle distances (NY-London actual: 2997-2999 NM, calculated: 3007.68 NM - within expected accuracy).
Verified in verifier.py line 30: tol_percent = metadata.get('tolerance_percent', 1.0). Used in accuracy calculation at lines 104-113 for percentage difference check.
Verified in verifier.py line 31: tol_abs = metadata.get('tolerance_absolute', 0.5). Used in accuracy calculation at lines 104-113 for absolute difference check.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses three data files (route INI, engine curve CSV, and ground truth JSON) with paths /home/ga/Documents/VoyageData/ and /var/lib/bridgecommand/. All paths are correctly referenced in task metadata. Earth radius of 3440.06 NM is the correct rounded value for mean Earth radius (3440.065 NM). Haversine formula implementation is verified correct. Tolerance values (1.0% and 0.5 MT) are strictly enforced with OR logic - either absolute or percentage threshold must pass for full accuracy credit. All data is synthetically generated at task runtime via random route and engine curve generation.
